commands.review_summary:
The PR updates the TUI custom editor to ignore Kitty release events, decode German-layout AltGr CSI-u printable input, and add regression coverage plus a changelog entry.

Reproducibility: Partly yes. The AltGr path is source-reproducible because current main delegates printable CSI-u input to pi-tui 0.75.1, whose decoder rejects Alt/Ctrl-modified printable sequences; the duplicated plain printable examples still need a live German-layout Kitty/CSI-u run.
commands.review_followup: The remaining action is validation and branch maintenance, not an automated code repair: the contributor needs real behavior proof and the branch needs rebase before merge.

target.title: fix(tui): handle German AltGr input
target.body:
## Summary
- ignore Kitty printable key-release events in the TUI custom editor so release events cannot duplicate text
- decode German-layout AltGr Kitty CSI-u printable input when terminals report Ctrl+Alt plus a base-layout key
- add regression coverage for AltGr @/€, ordinary Alt-modified input, and printable release events

## Verification
- pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/tui/components/custom-editor.ts src/tui/components/custom-editor.test.ts src/tui/tui.ts src/tui/tui.test.ts CHANGELOG.md
- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/tui/components/custom-editor.test.ts src/tui/tui.test.ts
- pnpm check:changed
- git diff --check

## Not Run
- Live German keyboard / Kitty-terminal smoke test; this environment does not have that keyboard layout attached.

Fixes openclaw/openclaw#48897
target.author: jason-allen-oneal
